plugins.name: nav
plugins.description: Keep any codebase navigable as it grows — audit (assess + feasibility + deep legacy sweep via domain fan-out), refactor (verbatim move + test gates), sync (file-top headers), map (bilingual codebase HTML), plan (spec → grounded plan), do (execute a small behaviour-changing change, disciplined, no artifact), compose (author/restructure a prose document as a deep module). Language-agnostic. Built on Ousterhout's deep-module principles (deep modules via information hiding).

plugins.name: shape
plugins.description: Forward motion: give intent its form via real disposable instances (not descriptions), record decisions legibly, keep them current, and build them into running, verified code. Sibling to nav. Skills: mockup (render-to-decide), elicit (grounded grill → a thought), position (canon-authoring campaign → core docs), setup (scaffold a new project to a verified baseline — archetype references + stack principles), dogfood (use a built feature for real → friction + coverage gaps), align (decide next + render the plan), reconcile (prune stale notes), build (drive the plan's In-progress to done); doctor deferred. Language-agnostic.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Argument anatomy for research work — dissect one or more documents into Gap/Claim/Mechanism/Evidence skeletons, untangle how a set of sources relate (lineage / clusters / contradictions / contested ground, with optional claim-framed positioning), critique a paper adversarially into a referee report (claim↔evidence audit + self-attack), or run a provenance audit over your own documents (trace every load-bearing citation back to a verified source; quarantine the unread).

plugins.name: frame
plugins.description: Apply an explicit, named frame that forces a structure the default skips — to a problem (four reasoning lenses: first-principles, orthogonal, dialectic, graft) or to an answer you already have (analogize — build a stress-tested analogy so it lands in plain language). Lenses feed shape one-way; analogize does not. Standalone. Renamed from think.

plugins.name: reflect
plugins.description: Reflect on your own working session — the one reflexive, cross-cutting family (the work process itself, not the code/decisions/docs/reasoning the other plugins operate on); it sits across the object-families, not parallel to them. Three seed skills, three time-stances on the same session: catchup (orient — where the work stands now + next, rebuilt from git/diff/plan, not chat memory), summarize (recap — a complete, objective account of what the session did), observe (distill — the one durable learning into a knowledge base). orient -> recap -> distill. Read-only except observe's write. Cross-project, standalone.

plugins.name: relay
plugins.description: Coordinate asynchronously with a counterpart through your agents over a shared git repo — structured updates, not chat; decisions reach explicit consensus; threads continue across cycles. Seven verbs: launch (create a project), register (enrol a person + role), report (a standup-shaped update), review (respond — agree / comment / change), digest (the live 'what's waiting for my review'), settle (append agreed thoughts to the decision ledger), format (sweep thought frontmatter to the current spec). git-native, human-gated, designed for multi-person. Language-agnostic.
